_emit_rules.purpose: Fillable template for Stage -0.5 (D-0.5) proposal-reviewer stdout JSON. The agent reads this file, fills placeholders, and emits exactly one JSON object as the last non-empty line of stdout. Field semantics, verdict routing, and the S / N / C flag codes are defined in stage_neg1_review.txt and are NOT duplicated here.
how_to_fill: Replace every string value whose payload starts with 'TODO:' with the actual content. The text after 'TODO:' is the inline hint for that slot.
Replace every 0-valued integer placeholder with the real count.
Each array contains exactly ONE prototype object. Duplicate it per real item, fill TODOs. If the result is empty for that array, emit [] (drop the prototype).
Drop every key whose name starts with '_' (this '_emit_rules' block and every '_prototype' marker inside arrays) before emitting.
Validity: single JSON object, last non-empty line of stdout, no prose wrapper, no markdown fences.

verdict: TODO: 'ACCEPT' | 'REVISE' | 'REJECT' per the VERDICT routing rules in stage_neg1_review.txt

structure_check._doc: every key here is a boolean; set false if the structural invariant is violated

per_theorem_calls._prototype: one entry per section8 numbered result (Theorem / Conjecture / Open-ended Question)
per_theorem_calls.label: TODO: 'Theorem 1' | 'Conjecture 1' | 'Open-ended Question 1' | ...

structure_flags._prototype: emit one entry per structural defect; emit [] when none. Code list lives in stage_neg1_review.txt.
structure_flags.label: TODO: section / result / paragraph the flag targets
structure_flags.one_line: TODO: <= one sentence diagnosing the defect

novelty_flags._prototype: one entry per novelty defect; emit [] when none. Full code list with semantics lives in stage_neg1_review.txt.
novelty_flags.label: TODO: target label
novelty_flags.code: TODO: one of N-repo | N-pub | N-mischar | N-thin-survey | N-all-theorem-trap | N-strawman | N-witness-trivial | N-no-implication | N-comparator-drift | N-thin-anchor | N-no-named-focal-object | N-no-comparator-chain | N-no-estimator-duality | N-no-matching-converse | N-no-concrete-witness | N-promissory-object

soundness_flags._prototype: one entry per soundness defect; emit [] when none. Full code list in stage_neg1_review.txt.
soundness_flags.label: TODO: target label
soundness_flags.code: TODO: one of C-wellposed | C-sanity | C-coherence | C-proof-sketch | C-scope | C-tautological-iff | C-free-epsilon | C-definitional-unfold

accepted_scope_caveats._prototype: one entry per §11 honest-scope flag you ACCEPT as a conditional claim (assumed-not-proved / not-a-construction / downgraded-to-premise). Stage 0.5 reads this list and treats these as settled scope, NOT fresh correctness defects. Emit [] when none accepted (or when you instead REVISE to force resolution).
accepted_scope_caveats.label: TODO: the §8/§11 target or assumption the caveat attaches to
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: TODO: one line naming what is assumed rather than proved
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: TODO: the conditional form the headline is now bound to (e.g. 'sharp UNDER the saturation convention')

publishability_tier: TODO: 'flagship' | 'field' | 'letter' | 'not-publishable' per the PUBLISHABILITY TIER section

strengthening_paths._prototype: REQUIRED regardless of verdict; minimum 2 distinct actionable suggestions
strengthening_paths.suggestion: TODO: one actionable suggestion (tighten assumption / sharpen conjecture / add corollary / quantify rate / name missed prior art / point at repo infrastructure)

recommended_next_step: TODO: one-line summary of what the orchestrator should do next (continue revise on this angle / pivot / advance to Stage 0 / escalate to user)
